content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of NNN integers - A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.

Count the number of arrays BBB, also of length NNN, satisfying the following conditions:


1‚Č§Bi‚Č§Ai1 \le B_i \le A_i1‚Č§Bi‚Äč‚Č§Ai‚Äč for all 1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \le i \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N.
Bi‚Č†BjB_i \ne B_jBi‚ÄčÓÄ†=Bj‚Äč for each 1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N1 \le i < j \le N1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N.
Since the answer may be large, output it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN - the size of the array AAA.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of arrays BBB possible modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : There are 444 arrays following the constraints, listed below:

B=[1,2]B = [1, 2]B=[1,2]
B=[2,1]B = [2, 1]B=[2,1]
B=[3,1]B = [3, 1]B=[3,1]
B=[3,2]B = [3, 2]B=[3,2]

Test Case 2 : There are no valid arrays.
Test Case 3 : Remember to output the answer modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
